summary: DHH expresses his love for the classic game Bubble Bobble and his excitement at being able to play it on a mobile phone via a C64 emulator. However, his enthusiasm is quickly crushed when he discovers that the Nokia 3650 cannot handle diagonal inputs, making the game unplayable. The post is a brief, lighthearted lament about early mobile gaming limitations.
key_points: Bubble Bobble is praised as a timeless classic that remains fun decades after its creation
The C64 emulator Frodo combined with C64.com enabled mobile retro gaming
The Nokia 3650's lack of diagonal input support ruined the mobile Bubble Bobble experience
money_quotes: Bubble Bobble is one of those golden classics that age gracefully and remains fun more than a decade since it was concieved.
The joy took a very disappointing end right as I discovered that the Nokia 3650 doesn't do diagonals.
key_insight: Early mobile hardware limitations could completely undermine the promise of portable retro gaming, no matter how good the emulation software was.
